intro: Compound units measure one quantity that involves two different units — like speed (distance per time) or density (mass per volume). Triangle diagrams help you recall and rearrange these formulas.
explanation:
Speed, Distance and Time:
Formula: Speed = Distance / Time.
Rearranged: Distance = Speed x Time. Time = Distance / Speed.
Units: speed in m/s or km/h, distance in m or km, time in seconds or hours.

Converting speed:
1 m/s = 3.6 km/h.
Multiply m/s by 3.6 to get km/h. Divide km/h by 3.6 to get m/s.

Example: A car travels 150 km in 2.5 hours. Speed = 150 / 2.5 = 60 km/h.

Density, Mass and Volume:
Formula: Density = Mass / Volume.
Rearranged: Mass = Density x Volume. Volume = Mass / Density.
Units: density in g/cm cubed or kg/m cubed.

Example: An object has mass 80 g and volume 10 cm cubed. Density = 80 / 10 = 8 g/cm cubed.

Pressure, Force and Area:
Formula: Pressure = Force / Area.
Rearranged: Force = Pressure x Area. Area = Force / Pressure.
Units: pressure in N/m squared (Pascals), force in Newtons, area in m squared.

Example: A force of 120 N acts on an area of 0.4 m squared. Pressure = 120 / 0.4 = 300 Pa.

worked_example.question: A train travels at 90 km/h for 1 hour 30 minutes. How far does it travel? Give your answer in km.

common_mistakes: Using inconsistent units — if speed is in km/h, time must be in hours and distance in km; do not mix units.
Dividing when you should multiply (or vice versa) — write the formula triangle and use it to rearrange before substituting.
Converting time wrongly — 1 hour 20 minutes is 1.333... hours (not 1.20 hours); divide minutes by 60 to get the decimal.
